title: myosotis scorpioides subsp. arvensis
intro: Myosotis scorpioides subsp. arvensis, commonly known as the field forget-me-not, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Myosotis scorpioides subsp. arvensis can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
